title: stelis laminata
intro: Stelis laminata, commonly known as "Wild Orchid" due to its orchid-like appearance, is a flowering plant species native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting the body from oxidative stress. The therapeutic actions of Stelis laminata include its potential to act as a vasodilator, improving blood circulation and lowering blood pressure, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which may help prevent infections. The bioactive constituents responsible for its therapeutic actions are flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been identified as the primary active compounds in the plant.
benefits: Stelis laminata has many benefits, such as its leaves being used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant's extracts have also been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, and its antioxidants can protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Furthermore, Stelis laminata has been used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and its anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. By incorporating Stelis laminata into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved physical and mental health, leading to a better quality of life and increased energy levels.
intro_study: According to "Frontiers in bioscience (Landmark edition)", stelis laminata, being part of the Lamiaceae family, may contribute to the prevention of cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, and cancer due to its potential use as a food or food additive.
parts: Stelis laminata has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds. The leaves of Stelis laminata are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of this plant contain iridoid glycosides, including stelisin and stelisin-7-O-β-D-glucopyranoside, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, the flowers of Stelis laminata contain coumarins, such as scopoletin and umbelliferone, which have been reported to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Stelis laminata can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts can also lead to gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In severe cases, using Stelis laminata without proper guidance can cause liver damage and other organ-related problems. Additionally, improper use of the plant can interact with other medications, resulting in adverse reactions.
